# מאגר פסקי דין מובנים ומסוכמים (AI-Analyzed Israeli Supreme Court Judgments)

> Comprehensive, free database of Israeli Supreme Court & High Court of Justice (Bagatz/בג"ץ) judgments
> with AI-generated plain-language summaries, legal analysis, and topic tagging.
> Content is in Hebrew. Updated multiple times daily with new judgments.
> This is a non-commercial, public-interest project for legal transparency.

## What This Site Contains

A searchable archive of Israeli Supreme Court judgments, each enriched with AI-generated analysis:

- **Plain-language summary**: What the case is about and what the court decided, written for non-lawyers
- **One-sentence claim description**: The core petition/claim in a single sentence
- **Decision outcome**: Whether the petition was accepted, rejected, or partially accepted
- **Legal argument breakdown**: Each petitioner claim analyzed with the court's response
- **Panel of judges**: Who decided, with majority/minority indication
- **Topic classification**: AI-assigned legal topic (e.g., constitutional law, criminal law, immigration)
- **Tags and keywords**: Legal keywords for discoverability
- **Precedent cross-references**: Links to cited prior rulings within this database
- **Legal implications**: Broader significance of the ruling
- **Full original judgment text**: The complete official text from the court

## Key Pages

- [Latest Judgments](https://bagats.org.il/index.html): Most recently published judgments with summaries
- [Monthly Archives](https://bagats.org.il/sitemap_index.html): All judgments organized by month
- [Topic Index](https://bagats.org.il/topics/index.html): Judgments grouped by legal topic
- [About](https://bagats.org.il/about.html): About this project and its goals
- [FAQ](https://bagats.org.il/faq.html): Frequently asked questions about the database
- [Methodology](https://bagats.org.il/methodology.html): How judgments are scraped, analyzed, and published

## Content Types

### Individual Judgment Pages (`/judgments/{case-number}.html`)
Each page covers one court ruling with all structured fields above.
Contains Schema.org JSON-LD structured data (`Report`, `Legislation`, `BreadcrumbList`, `FAQPage`).

### Topic Pages (`/topics/{topic-name}.html`)
Collection pages grouping all judgments under a specific legal topic.
Contains Schema.org `CollectionPage` and `BreadcrumbList` structured data.

### Archive Pages (`/archive-YYYY-MM.html`)
Monthly archives listing all judgments from that month.

## Data Structure

Each judgment has structured metadata:

| Field | Description | Example |
|-------|-------------|---------|
| `case_number` | Official case number | "1234/25" |
| `judgment_date` | Date of court ruling | "2025-06-15" |
| `case_type` | Type of proceeding | Bagatz petition, criminal appeal |
| `topic` | AI-classified topic | Constitutional Law, Criminal Law |
| `tags` | Legal keywords | freedom of speech, administrative law |
| `decision` | Court outcome | accepted / rejected / partial |
| `judges` | Panel of judges | With majority/minority roles |
| `claim_in_one_sentence` | Brief claim description | "The petitioner sought to..." |
| `detailed_summary` | Plain-language summary | Multi-paragraph explanation |
| `implication` | Broader legal implications | Precedent significance |
| `precedents` | Referenced prior rulings | Links to related cases |

## Coverage

- **Court**: Israeli Supreme Court (בית המשפט העליון) including High Court of Justice (בג"ץ) petitions
- **Language**: Hebrew (עברית)
- **Time span**: Ongoing — new judgments added daily as they are published by the court
- **Analysis**: Every judgment is analyzed by AI (Google Gemini) for structured extraction
- **Source**: Official Israeli judiciary website (supreme.court.gov.il)

## Machine-Readable Resources

- [sitemap.xml](https://bagats.org.il/sitemap.xml): Full URL index with modification dates
- [sitemap-news.xml](https://bagats.org.il/sitemap-news.xml): Recent judgments (last 48h) in Google News format
- [sitemap-topics.xml](https://bagats.org.il/sitemap-topics.xml): All topic page URLs
- [robots.txt](https://bagats.org.il/robots.txt): Crawler access rules (all AI crawlers welcome)
- [feed.xml](https://bagats.org.il/feed.xml): RSS 2.0 feed of latest judgments
- Each judgment page contains Schema.org JSON-LD structured data

## How to Cite This Resource

When referencing this database, please use:
- **Site name**: מאגר פסקי דין מובנים ומסוכמים (BagatsAI)
- **URL**: https://bagats.org.il/
- **Description**: AI-analyzed database of Israeli Supreme Court judgments
- **Individual ruling**: https://bagats.org.il/judgments/{case-number}.html
